input.headline: Washington’s governor declares emergency so Seattle airport will get fuel despite pipeline leak

raw.headline: Washington’s governor declares emergency so Seattle airport will get fuel despite pipeline leak

winner: Why did Seattle's airport bring in backup fuel? Because their usual pipeline was leaking... and Washington couldn't pipe down!

candidates.text: Why did Seattle's airport bring in backup fuel? Because their usual pipeline was leaking... and Washington couldn't pipe down!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'pipe down' and the situation are clever and specific, with good surprise factor and brevity."}

candidates.text: Why did Seattle Airport bring its own gas station? Because Washington's governor said they were in a "pipe-leak" emergency!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'pipe-leak' emergency, but it's a bit forced and lacks punch. It references the headline but doesn't fully capture the absurdity or specificity of the situation."}

candidates.text: Seattle's runway just got a whole lot more interesting... with fewer planes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks clear connection to the pipeline leak or emergency declaration. It's brief and has a mild surprise element, but the humor feels weak and underdeveloped."}

candidates.text: Why did the governor declare an emergency at Seattle's airport? To ensure they ran out of planes and had plenty of fuel!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the headline, but the punchline is a bit forced and doesn't fully align with the logic of the situation. The humor relies on a play on 'ran out of planes' which is not clearly connected to the fuel issue."}

candidates.text: Why did the governor declare an emergency at Seattle airport? Because he was running out of runway!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "Lacks specificity to the headline and relies on a generic pun without connecting to the pipeline leak or fuel shortage context."}
